# bankruptcykansascity.org -- Full Content

This file contains the full text content of all pages on bankruptcykansascity.org.
Part of the Open Bankruptcy Project (openbankruptcyproject.org).


---

## Kansas City Bankruptcy Statistics - Chapter 13 Dismissal Rates [2026]
Source: https://bankruptcykansascity.org/

Skip to main content

Bankruptcy Kansas City
HomeStatisticsWhat to KnowResourcesOBP Rule 9037 PII Scanner

Kansas City Bankruptcy Statistics - Chapter 13 Dismissal Data

Kansas City straddles two federal districts with notably different Chapter 13 outcomes. The data below compares the Western District of Missouri and the District of Kansas side by side.

W.D. Mo.: 93.5% preliminary dismissal rate in 2024*

40.4%

WDMO Rate (2008-2019)

46,548

WDMO Cases Filed (2008-2024)

31.4%

D. Kansas Rate

44,949

D. Kansas Cases

93.5%

WDMO 2024 Rate

4,555

Est. 1328(f) Violations

WDMO Dismissal Rate by Year (2008--2024)

Chapter 13 Dismissal Rate - Western District of Missouri

Source: Federal Judicial Center Integrated Database, 2008--2024. Analysis by Open Bankruptcy Project.

Methodology note: Chapter 13 plans run 3-5 years. Cases filed 2020-2024 are preliminary - successful plans are still active, so only early dismissals are reflected. The headline rate uses 2008-2019 data where most cases have reached final disposition. Source: Federal Judicial Center Integrated Database.

Two Districts, Two Outcomes

Western District of Missouri

40.4%

46,548 cases filed - 16,844 dismissed, 21,878 discharged. 28.6% prior filer rate. 3.8% pro se. An estimated 2,134 potential 1328(f) violations. Preliminary 2024 rate: 93.5% (cases still maturing).

Kansas City MO (400 E. 9th St)

District of Kansas

31.4%

44,949 cases filed - 11,748 dismissed, 25,615 discharged. 26.1% prior filer rate. 1.6% pro se. An estimated 2,421 potential 1328(f) violations.

Kansas City KS (500 State Ave)

The Kansas City Divide

Side-by-Side Comparison

Kansas City consumers on opposite sides of State Line Road see different Chapter 13 outcomes in the data:

Missouri side: 40.4% overall dismissal rate; preliminary 2024 rate of 93.5%

Kansas side: 31.4% overall dismissal rate, relatively stable over time

Missouri prior filer rate: 28.6%

Missouri trend: Rose from 35.5% in 2010 to 93.5% in preliminary 2024 data

The Missouri side's rate increased significantly between 2020 and 2023. However, recent-year rates are preliminary because Chapter 13 plans run 3-5 years and many successful cases are still active.

Estimated 1328(f) Violations

Across both districts, an estimated 4,555 cases may involve potential 1328(f) eligibility issues - filings where the debtor had a prior discharge within the statutory time bar. In these cases, a new discharge may not be available under current law.

Frequently Asked Questions

What is the Chapter 13 dismissal rate in Kansas City?

Kansas City straddles two federal districts. The Western District of Missouri has a 40.4% dismissal rate based on mature 2008-2019 data. The District of Kansas has a healthier 31.4% rate. Recent years show higher preliminary rates because successful 3-5 year plans are still active.

Why is the Missouri side worse than Kansas?

W.D. Mo. dismissal rates have risen from 35.5% in 2010 to preliminary rates above 90% in 2023-2024. The District of Kansas has remained more stable. Contributing factors include differences in local rules, trustee practices, and attorney quality.

How many Chapter 13 cases are filed in Kansas City?

Between 2008 and 2024, W.D. Mo. saw 46,548 Chapter 13 filings and D. Kansas saw 44,949, for a combined 91,497 cases across the metro area.

What is a 1328(f) violation?

Under 11 U.S.C. section 1328(f), debtors who received a prior discharge within specific timeframes cannot receive another discharge. An estimated 2,134 W.D. Mo. cases and 2,421 D. Kansas cases may involve potential violations.

Should I file on the Kansas or Missouri side?

Bankruptcy venue is determined by where you live, not by choice. However, the data underscores the importance of strong legal representation, especially on the Missouri side. Consult a qualified attorney who practices in your jurisdiction.

Check Your Eligibility

Use the free 1328(f) screener to check whether a prior discharge affects your eligibility.

Need Free Bankruptcy Help?

If you cannot afford a bankruptcy attorney, free help is available. Legal aid organizations, law school clinics, and court self-help programs can assist you at no cost.

## Kansas City Bankruptcy Resources - Courts, Legal Aid, Free Tools | Bankruptcy Kansas City
Source: https://bankruptcykansascity.org/resources.html

Bankruptcy Kansas City
HomeStatisticsWhat to KnowResourcesOBP

Home›Kansas City Bankruptcy Resources - Courts, Legal Aid, Free Tools | Bankruptcy Kansas City›Kansas City Bankruptcy Resources

Kansas City Bankruptcy Resources

Courts, legal aid organizations, free tools, and research resources for Kansas City area bankruptcy filers.

Federal Bankruptcy Courts

Western District of Missouri

mow.uscourts.gov

Kansas City Division: 400 E. 9th Street, Kansas City, MO 64106. Also serves Springfield, Jefferson City, and St. Joseph divisions.

District of Kansas

ksb.uscourts.gov

Kansas City Division: 500 State Avenue, Kansas City, KS 66101. Also serves Topeka and Wichita divisions.

Legal Aid and Pro Bono Services

Legal Aid of Western Missouri

lawmo.org

Free civil legal services for low-income residents in western Missouri, including bankruptcy assistance. Multiple offices in the KC metro.

Kansas Legal Services

kansaslegalservices.org

Free legal help for Kansans who qualify financially. Offices in Kansas City KS, Topeka, and Wichita.

Kansas City Metropolitan Bar Association - Lawyer Referral Service

kcmba.org

Referral service connecting consumers with attorneys. Initial consultations available at reduced cost.

UMKC School of Law - Legal Clinics

law.umkc.edu

Law school clinics providing supervised legal assistance in various practice areas.

Free Screening and Research Tools

1328(f) Discharge Eligibility Screener

1328f.com

Free tool to check whether a prior discharge creates a time bar under 11 U.S.C. section 1328(f). Runs entirely in your browser - no data collected.

Open Bankruptcy Project

openbankruptcyproject.org

National research platform analyzing Chapter 13 outcomes across all 94 federal districts.

1328(f) Research Platform

1328f.org

In-depth research on repeat filing patterns and 1328(f) eligibility violations.

Federal Resources

U.S. Trustee Program - Region 13

justice.gov/ust

Oversees bankruptcy case administration in W.D. Mo. File complaints about attorney or trustee misconduct.

PACER - Public Access to Court Electronic Records

pacer.uscourts.gov

Access federal court records including bankruptcy dockets. Fee-based ($0.10/page, capped at $3.00/document).

Federal Judicial Center - Integrated Database

fjc.gov/research/idb

The source database for all statistics on this site.

State Bar Associations

Missouri Bar - Attorney Complaint

mobar.org

File complaints about Missouri-licensed attorneys through the Office of Chief Disciplinary Counsel.

Kansas Disciplinary Administrator

kscourts.org

File complaints about Kansas-licensed attorneys through the Kansas Disciplinary Administrator.

More from the OBP Network

Chapter 7 vs. Chapter 13

chapter7vs13.org

Side-by-side comparison to help understand which chapter may be appropriate.

Bankruptcy Dismissed - What Now?

bankruptcydismissed.com

Information for people whose case was dismissed - next steps and options.

Free, open-source bankruptcy transparency. No ads. No affiliate links. Supported by donations.

An Open Bankruptcy Project resource

This is general information, not legal advice. Consult a qualified attorney.

## WDMO Chapter 13 Statistics - Year-by-Year Data | Bankruptcy Kansas City
Source: https://bankruptcykansascity.org/statistics.html

Bankruptcy Kansas City
HomeStatisticsWhat to KnowResourcesOBP

Home›WDMO Chapter 13 Statistics - Year-by-Year Data | Bankruptcy Kansas City›Kansas City Chapter 13 Statistics - Complete Data

Kansas City Chapter 13 Statistics - Complete Data

Year-by-year breakdown of Chapter 13 bankruptcy outcomes in the Western District of Missouri and District of Kansas. All data sourced from the Federal Judicial Center Integrated Database.

40.4%

WDMO Rate (2008-2019)

46,548

WDMO Total Cases

31.4%

D. Kansas Overall Rate

44,949

D. Kansas Total Cases

93.5%

WDMO 2024 Rate

4,555

Combined Est. Violations

Year-by-Year Breakdown - Western District of Missouri

YearCases FiledDismissal RatePrior Filer RateTrend

20083,42042.2%24.5%

20093,63736.4%22.1%-5.8

20104,27435.5%23.3%-0.9

20113,72839.2%24.2%+3.7

20123,41441.8%25.5%+2.6

20132,97242.5%27.9%+0.7

20142,67239.9%29.8%-2.6

20152,50744.0%31.8%+4.1

20162,46443.2%33.3%-0.8

20172,56242.4%34.3%-0.8

20182,69642.5%36.1%+0.1

20192,80939.1%32.6%-3.4

2020*2,08042.4%33.3%+3.3

2021*1,47857.1%30.0%+14.7

2022*1,79478.8%31.8%+21.7

2023*2,03692.3%30.9%+13.5

2024*2,00593.5%27.4%+1.2

* Preliminary - Chapter 13 plans run 3-5 years, so successful plans filed in these years are still active. Only early dismissals are reflected.

Methodology note: Chapter 13 plans run 3-5 years. Cases filed 2020-2024 are preliminary - successful plans are still active, so only early dismissals are reflected. The headline rate uses 2008-2019 data where most cases have reached final disposition. Source: Federal Judicial Center Integrated Database.

Rate Trend: 2020-2024

WDMO's dismissal rate was in the low 40s for over a decade (2008-2020). Preliminary data for recent years shows a sharp increase:

2020: 42.4%

2021: 57.1% (preliminary)

2022: 78.8% (preliminary)

2023: 92.3% (preliminary)

2024: 93.5% (preliminary)

These recent rates are preliminary because Chapter 13 plans run 3-5 years. Many cases filed in 2020-2024 are still in active repayment and have not yet reached final disposition. The mature 2008-2019 rate of 40.4% provides a more reliable baseline.

Prior Filer Analysis

The prior filer rate in WDMO peaked at 36.1% in 2018 but has declined in recent years (27.4% in 2024). This suggests the rising preliminary dismissal rate is not driven by an increase in repeat filers.

In D. Kansas, the prior filer rate has remained around 26%, and the dismissal rate has stayed in the low 30s. The two districts serve similar metro populations but show different outcome patterns.

District of Kansas Comparison

D. Kansas has maintained relatively stable outcomes throughout the same period:

Overall dismissal rate: 31.4%

44,949 total cases - similar volume to WDMO

25,615 discharges (57.0% success rate)

Only 1.6% pro se rate vs. 3.8% in WDMO

26.1% prior filer rate vs. 28.6% in WDMO

The Kansas side shows different outcome patterns with a comparable filing population. The reasons for the divergence may include differences in local rules, trustee practices, and attorney representation patterns.

Explore the full methodology and national data at the Open Bankruptcy Project.

Visit OBP

Free, open-source bankruptcy transparency. No ads. No affiliate links. Supported by donations.

An Open Bankruptcy Project resource

Data: Federal Judicial Center Integrated Database (2008--2024).

## What to Know About Chapter 13 in Kansas City - Consumer Guide | Bankruptcy Kansas City
Source: https://bankruptcykansascity.org/what-to-know.html

Bankruptcy Kansas City
HomeStatisticsWhat to KnowResourcesOBP

Home›What to Know About Chapter 13 in Kansas City - Consumer Guide | Bankruptcy Kansas City›What Kansas City Consumers Should Know About Chapter 13

What Kansas City Consumers Should Know About Chapter 13

If you are considering Chapter 13 bankruptcy in the Kansas City metro - or have already been dismissed - this page explains what the data means for you.

Missouri Side: Rising Preliminary Rates

The Western District of Missouri's preliminary dismissal rate rose from 42.4% in 2020 to 93.5% in 2024. Recent-year rates are preliminary because many plans are still active. If you are filing on the Missouri side, understanding local outcomes can help you prepare.

What Does "Dismissed" Mean?

When a Chapter 13 case is dismissed, the repayment plan failed and the case was thrown out. You do not receive a discharge of your debts. Common causes include:

Missed plan payments - failing to make required monthly payments to the trustee

Failure to file required documents - tax returns, pay stubs, certificates

Plan not confirmable - the proposed plan does not meet legal requirements

Ineligibility - the debtor was never eligible (including 1328(f) time bars)

Voluntary dismissal - the debtor requests dismissal

Dismissal means creditors can resume collection. You have spent time and money with no debt relief.

The State Line Matters

Kansas City is unusual because the state line creates two completely different bankruptcy systems:

Missouri side (WDMO): 93.5% preliminary dismissal rate in 2024 (cases still maturing)

Kansas side (D. Kan.): 31.4% overall dismissal rate (2008-2019 mature data)

You file in the district where you live - you cannot choose. But understanding your district's track record is essential before committing to a 3-5 year plan.

Questions to Ask an Attorney Before Filing

1. What is your firm's Chapter 13 completion rate in this district?

Ask how their clients' completion rates compare to the district average. An attorney who tracks this data can help set realistic expectations.

2. Have you checked my eligibility under 1328(f)?

If you have a prior discharge, statutory time bars may prevent you from receiving another one. Your attorney must screen for this before filing.

3. Why Chapter 13 instead of Chapter 7?

Chapter 7 cases complete in months with much higher success rates. Make sure your attorney explains specifically why Chapter 13 is recommended for your situation.

4. How many cases does your firm file per month?

High-volume practices may not provide the individual attention needed to navigate a 3-5 year plan. Ask what ongoing support you will receive after filing.

5. What happens to my fees if the case is dismissed?

Most Chapter 13 attorney fees are paid through the plan. If dismissed, the attorney may have been paid while you received no benefit.

6. What is your plan to prevent dismissal?

Your attorney should have a specific strategy for keeping your case on track through the full 3-5 year plan period.

Red Flags to Watch For

Warning Signs

No eligibility screening - no check of prior filing history before accepting your case

Limited attorney contact - you do not meet the attorney who signs your petition

No discussion of Chapter 7 - Chapter 13 presented as the only option

No explanation of the district's dismissal rate - your attorney should be transparent about the environment

Unreachable after filing - once the case is filed, you cannot get responses

Multiple prior dismissals - if you have failed before, the same firm and approach is unlikely to work

If You Have Been Dismissed

Review the dismissal order - understand the specific reason your case failed

Check time bars - sections 109(g) and 362(c) may limit your automatic stay if you refile

Evaluate your attorney - did they file documents on time? Communicate with you? Attend hearings?

Consider alternatives - Chapter 7, debt negotiation, or waiting may be better than refiling

Get a second opinion - if the same firm wants to refile, consult a different attorney first

Check Your Eligibility - Free Screener

Free, open-source bankruptcy transparency. No ads. No affiliate links. Supported by donations.

An Open Bankruptcy Project resource

This is general information, not legal advice. Consult a qualified attorney.

## Privacy Policy - bankruptcykansascity.org
Source: https://bankruptcykansascity.org/privacy.html

bankruptcykansascity.org

Home
Privacy

Privacy Policy

How we handle your data. Short version: we don't sell it, share it, or use it for advertising.

Data We Collect

We use Google Analytics (GA4) to measure aggregate site traffic. This includes page views, session duration, approximate geographic region (country and city level), and device type. We do not collect names, email addresses, or personally identifiable information through analytics.

Aggregate event data from Google Analytics is also exported to Google BigQuery for research use. The BigQuery data is used only for internal trajectory analysis and network health reports, is not shared with third parties, and is not used for advertising.

What We Don't Do

We do not sell data. We do not run advertising. We do not collect financial information. We do not use retargeting. We do not share any user data with attorneys, law firms, creditors, debt collectors, or legal service providers.

Specifically, we do not enable Google Signals, Google Ads integration, User-ID collection, or any cross-device tracking feature in Google Analytics. GA4 operates in aggregate-traffic-measurement mode only on this site.

Cookies

We use only first-party cookies set by Google Analytics for aggregate traffic measurement. We do not use advertising cookies, tracking pixels, or third-party analytics cookies.

Third-Party Services

Google Analytics (GA4) - aggregate traffic measurement

Google BigQuery - aggregate event data warehouse for research use only; not shared, not used for advertising

Google Search Console - search query measurement (clicks, impressions, our own page rankings)

GitHub Pages - hosting

Contact Forms & Email

This site may link to contact forms or email addresses operated by the Open Bankruptcy Project. Any data you submit through those channels is handled under the Open Bankruptcy Project master privacy policy, which governs all sites in this network.

Children's Privacy

This site is not directed at children under 13. We do not knowingly collect information from children.

Data Retention

Analytics data is retained for 14 months per Google's standard retention settings. After that window, individual event data is automatically deleted by Google; aggregate report metrics remain available.

Your Rights

You may request deletion of any personal data we hold by contacting info@openbankruptcyproject.org. California residents have additional rights under CCPA. EU residents have additional rights under GDPR. All such requests are handled by the Open Bankruptcy Project.

Network Policy

This site is part of the Bankruptcy Transparency Network operated by the Open Bankruptcy Project, a 501(c)(3) nonprofit. The same privacy standards apply across every site in the network. The authoritative master policy is maintained at https://openbankruptcyproject.org/privacy.html.

Changes

We may update this policy from time to time. Changes are reflected on the master policy first and then mirrored across all network sites. Check the master policy for the most current version.

Effective: March 30, 2026  |  Updated: April 11, 2026

Home  ·  Privacy  ·  Open Bankruptcy Project

Part of the Bankruptcy Transparency Network. Educational information only - not legal advice.

No ads. No affiliate links. No data sold. Supported by donations.
